promotion_tool: governed dev→release promotion (copy allow-listed changed files + ZIP)
store_upload: NONE — no Chrome Web Store upload performed by this promotion
github_release: NONE — no GitHub Release created by this promotion
wildcard_host_permissions: NONE — AG-363 removed https://*/* from manifest.chrome.json and manifest.firefox.json (unchanged by this promotion)
note: Governed dev→release promotion of AG-PROMPT-383 (medical/health false-positive fix on technical diagnosis reports) on top of the AG-382 baseline, which already promoted AG-381 (HR/Employee precision fix) and, before that, AG-378 + AG-379 (PDF Author metadata precedence + field-blind metadata confidential fix) into ZIP SHA 2ad3b5a4...; none of those are re-described here as pending — they are already shipped in the prior release state and remain unchanged and confirmed present in this promotion. Exactly two allow-listed files changed since the AG-382 promotion lineage (source commit c591ec2): src/detection/packs/english.ts and src/detection/packs/global.ts (confirmed via git diff against c591ec2, restricted to the full AG-269 allow-list, and independently via a full recursive src/ tree diff between dev and release before this promotion — no other allow-listed file touched; public/manifest.chrome.json and public/manifest.firefox.json confirmed byte-identical between dev and release, no permission drift). AG-383 root cause (TECHNICAL_DIAGNOSIS_FALSE_POSITIVE, primary; MEDICAL_DIAGNOSIS_KEYWORD_TOO_WEAK, compounding): a technical website/deployment debugging report (Cloudflare Pages, CSP headers, inline styles, root-cause analysis) fired Medical Content / Health information warnings, including a CRITICAL-severity hard-floor PHI warning, purely from the word 'diagnosis'/'diagnose' used in its ordinary software sense. english-health-phi (english.ts) had NO minimum-match-count requirement at all — a single bare 'diagnosis' anywhere fired the most serious warning class this system produces. registry-medical-content (global.ts) already had minCount:2 from prior hardening, but that mechanism counts raw matches, not distinct evidence — a technical report repeating 'diagnosis'/'diagnose' 2+ times (which such reports naturally do) satisfied minCount:2 using only that one ambiguous, non-clinical word with zero genuine clinical corroboration. Fixed by removing bare 'diagnosis'/'diagnose' from both patterns' keyword alternations; every other keyword (medical record, patient id, treatment plan, prescription, health insurance, hipaa, patient journal, health record, clinical note, lab result, blood test, medication, prognosis, plus Nordic/DE/FR/IT/NL equivalents) is specific/unambiguous and unaffected. Genuine clinical/PHI documents verifiably continue to fire both signals, confirmed via the pre-existing critical-severity PHI gold fixture (english-phi-note-paste.clipboard.json) plus 4 new synthetic positive controls. This is a detection-pattern precision fix only — no detection threshold structure, RiskSignal structure, deduplication, chunking logic, or severity architecture changed; no signal IDs added or removed; no NEW critical severity or hard floor added (the existing english-health-phi hardFloor is unchanged in kind, only its matching precision was tightened). AG-369/370/371/374/376/378/379/381 (previously promoted) remain included unchanged: handleDrop's event.stopImmediatePropagation() Copilot drag/drop Cancel fix, the Firefox Xray-wrapper realm-copy helper for DOCX/XLSX/PPTX extraction parity, the XLSX/PPTX extraction success-semantics fix, the nordic-financial-terms bare-'balance' false-positive fix, the global-dob spelled-out-month-date fix plus meta.author abbreviation/tool-name guard, the field-aware metadata-confidential fix, and the HR/employee minCount:2 + regex-g-flag fix — all confirmed present via a byte-identical comparison of this promotion's release-built content.js/background.js against a same-session dev-built Chrome bundle (strongest available fix-presence proof; confirms the entire src/ tree, not just the two changed files, matches dev exactly), plus literal-string greps for 'bonus' (AG-381), 'jan(?:uary)?' (AG-378), and 'stopImmediatePropagation' (AG-369). No manifest, permission, or host-scope change (permissions: ['storage'] only; host_permissions limited to the 6 listed patterns; no activeTab; no wildcard/https://*/*; no <all_urls>; connect-src 'none'). No telemetry, network diagnostics, or centralized logging added; no raw document content, file names, or personal data logged or persisted (production bundle strips all console.log/console.debug via terser pure_funcs, verified; zero new diagnostic calls in either changed file). No local reproduction file (docs/test-files/5.txt) existed for AG-383 — its reproduction was entirely synthetic; docs/test-files/1.txt through 4.txt (pre-existing local material from AG-376/AG-381) remain uncommitted and are not part of this or any promotion. Dev-side verification (this promotion): tsc 0 errors; targeted medical technical-diagnosis suite 20/20; targeted keyword-precision suite 30/30; AG-378 PDF Author metadata suite 15/15; AG-379 metadata confidential field-awareness suite 19/19; CORE 55/55; corpus gold harness 30/0/1-blocked (pre-existing, unrelated); clipboard gold harness 125/125 (incl. re-verified english-phi-note-paste critical PHI positive control and english-hr-compensation-paste positive control); signal-manifest-integrity 24/24 (no registration drift, 93 pack pattern IDs / 101 manifest constants unchanged); verify-manifest 28/28; Chrome build 34/34 + 20/20 consumer-copy; Firefox build 25/25. Release-side verification: release-verify.ts 10/10 gates (type-check, build:chrome + artifact/console-strip checks, package:chrome + no-maps/no-TS guards, license UX guardrail, no-activity-record, message-handler safety, storage manager, storage hardening, signal-manifest integrity, manifest verification); ZIP contents confirmed as exactly the 14 expected production files (no .map/.ts/reports/dev URLs/prompt IDs/docs-test-files). No store upload; no GitHub Release; no website repo modified.
